comps.notes: Most expensive Orlando MF transaction of 2024

transaction_velocity.notes: Orlando MF transaction activity increased 56% YoY in 2025. Quarterly transaction volume in Q3 2025 hit highest level since 2022. Median price per unit declined ~25% from 2022-2025, attracting new buyers. JLL actively marketing select parcels at Creative Village.

distress_level.notes: No significant distress signals found for Creative Village/Parramore multifamily. The broader Orlando market is healthy with cap rate compression and institutional buyers returning. No forced sales or receiverships reported in this submarket.

supply_risk.notes: Phase 2 is a $600M investment. Orlando metro added 30,000 units in the past 2 years. However, construction starts are tapering nationally and locally, which should moderate future supply risk. Unlocked Open-Door Program targets 300+ vacant lots for single-family (not competing with MF supply).

rent_growth.notes: Creative Village specifically saw -0.1% rent decline. Orlando metro forecast at 2.0-2.4% rent growth by year-end. Modera Creative Village offering up to 1 month free rent. Average asking rents: studio $1,561, 1BR $1,729, 2BR $2,222. Metro average asking rent at $1,763. Rent growth expected to accelerate in 2026-2027 as supply tappers.

demand_drivers.notes: Orlando is #1 fastest-growing major metro in the US. Added 76,000 residents in 2024 (1,500/week). 2.7% population growth, surpassing FL statewide rate. 65% of recent growth from international migration. Employment growth forecast at 1.3% in 2026 (vs 0.5% national). 1 million job openings projected 2026-2031. UCF and Valencia College anchor Creative Village with strong education pipeline. SunRail transit-oriented development. LYMMO lines connect Parramore to downtown.

capital_availability.notes: Multifamily lending projected to increase 21% to $399.2B in 2026. Fannie/Freddie allocated ~20% increase in capped business. CMBS issuance surged to $115.2B through Nov 2025, highest since 2007. AAA spreads much tighter than 2022-2023. Florida MF cap rates at 5.5% (vs 5.2% national). Cap rate compression expected in 2026 toward 5.0-5.5% range low end.

regulatory_favorability.notes: Florida state law preempts local rent control measures. Parramore is an active federal Opportunity Zone (South Parramore OZ). CRA actively supporting development — contributed debt to Parramore Oaks ($20M in tax credits/incentives). Unlocked Open-Door Program provides 100% rebate on impact/building permit fees. City allocated $750K CRA funds. $45K down payment assistance available. EPA cleanup grants funded. Orange County property tax rate ~0.94%. Florida 2026 property tax relief proposals pending.

key_findings.finding: Creative Village Phase 2 represents a $600M investment with 1,278 planned residential units (450 market-rate, 106 mixed-income, 600 student, 122 proposed), creating significant near-term supply that will pressure absorption
Orlando is the #1 fastest-growing major metro in the US across jobs, population, and GDP — the triple crown — with 76,000 new residents added in 2024 alone
Orlando MF transaction volume increased 56% YoY in 2025, with Q3 quarterly volume the highest since 2022. Median price per unit down ~25% from 2022 peak, creating attractive entry points

Creative Village rents slightly declining (-0.1% YoY) with concessions offered (1 month free at Modera). Metro rent growth forecast at 2.0-2.4% suggests submarket-specific softness from elevated deliveries

South Parramore is an active federal Opportunity Zone with proven OZ + LIHTC stacking (Parramore Oaks: $20M in tax credits). No rent control risk due to state preemption. City launched Unlocked program with permit fee rebates

Multifamily lending projected to increase 21% nationally to $399.2B in 2026. CMBS issuance at highest since 2007. Cap rate compression expected toward 5.0% range. Institutional buyers returning to Orlando
UnionWest at Creative Village sold in Dec 2024 as Orlando's most expensive MF transaction of the year, validating institutional demand for this specific submarket
SunRail Sunshine Corridor extension from Orlando Airport to Disney Springs could boost ridership from 1M to 6M+ annually, dramatically improving Creative Village's transit connectivity value

feature_gaps_identified.gap: Cannot access Orlando City permit database programmatically to pull actual building permit counts for Parramore submarket
No access to CoStar/RCA for actual sales comp data (price per unit, cap rates) for Creative Village transactions
Cannot pull lis pendens or foreclosure filing data for Orange County to quantify distress level
No CMBS delinquency rate data available at the MSA level for Orlando specifically
Rent by class (A/B/C) not available at the subzone level — only metro-wide estimates available
No automated pipeline to track zoning variances, PUD amendments, or impact fee changes from the Orlando city council agenda
Need submarket-level absorption rate calculation — overall Orlando occupancy trending up but Creative Village may differ due to concentrated new supply
Heat map visualization of transaction activity and rent levels by census tract within the zone would significantly improve spatial analysis
